summary:
The primary effect of nitric oxide involves activation of guanylate cyclase resulting in elevated cGMP; it does not predominantly act through cAMP as suggested. Key mechanisms include regulation of vasomotor function without exclusive reliance on cAMP signaling paths.
The main point regarding exceptions concerns its broad impact extending into areas such as immunity where effects may be mediated differently or involve indirect influences but do not hinge solely upon interactions within a specific pathway involving only one second messenger molecule type at core level functionality. To directly answer your query based strictly around central tenets defining basic operational principles—certain aspects might indeed extend further yet remain outside scope dictated purely by single-pathway dominance over others thus making initial assumption overly restrictive when evaluating overall complexity involved across multiple systems simultaneously impacted albeit possibly less prominently emphasized compared against more immediate targets responsive mainly due their proximity relative importance given context constraints present during actual therapeutic application
